global_rules.description: Professional Windows utility-style dashboard for mobile repair (Aether Repair Tool). Focus on density, technical aesthetics, and high-contrast terminal elements.

window_chrome.description: Fake Windows desktop title bar at the top with App Name and Window Controls (Min, Max, Close).

device_status.visuals: Large typography, blinking cursor or pulse animation for 'Searching...', solid green for 'Device Detected'.

device_info_panel.style: Dense table, 1px borders, monospace font for data values (IMEI, Serial).

buttons.style: Flat, sharp edges, 1px border border-[#00FF41]/30 hover:bg-[#00FF41]/10 text-white hover:text-[#00FF41] transition-colors

console.style: Retro-Futurism (Terminal Look). bg-[#050505] border border-white/10. Monospace text. Auto-scrolling to bottom.
lines: Color code levels: [INFO] cyan, [SUCCESS] green, [WARN] yellow, [ERROR] red

animations.console_scroll: Smooth snap to bottom as new logs appear
animations.hover: Instant background tint change (0.1s ease) for buttons to feel highly responsive

media_and_images.instructions: No external photos required. Use SVGs and Phosphor Icons (@phosphor-icons/react) or Lucide for technical icons (USB port, microchip, terminal, settings). Ensure icon strokes are sharp and align with the monospace/technical aesthetic.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install `lucide-react` for icons if not present. Use sharp, technical icons.
Simulate device connection logic using a `useEffect` and `setTimeout` to transition from 'Searching...' to 'Device Detected: MT6895'.
Generate random mock data for IMEI, Serial, and Android version during the simulated connection.
Implement the console as a scrollable `div` that receives log append events. Create a custom hook `useTerminal` to manage log state.
Ensure the entire app is wrapped in a container that mimics a desktop window (100vh height, overflow hidden, custom scrollbars).
